content.full_text:
Los Angeles Unified School District

ANNUITY RESERVE FUND

MEMBERS OF THE BOARD
DOROTHY J. MOSER, Chairperson
GERALD REEDY, Vice-Chairperson
RICHARD BELL
KEN COYNE
SANDRA M. DAVIS
JACKIE GOLDBERG
ROBERT W. NICHOLS

Business Services Center
1425 SOUTH SAN PEDRO STREET
P.O. BOX 2298
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90051
(213) 742-7988

WILLIAM R. ANTON
Superintendent of Schools

ROBERT BOOKER
Chief Business and Financial Officer

April 2, 1991

Ms. Blanca M. Breeze
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Breeze:

In accordance with the first page of your communication of December 5, 1990 (copy enclosed), I am not certain as to whether our Annuity Reserve Fund Board members are required to file your Form 721, Statement of Economic Interest.

The School District's Annuity Reserve Fund was created in 1972 and is governed by a seven member Board of Directors. Four members of the Board are elected by the participants in the Fund, and must be participants themselves. The fifth member is the Los Angeles County Treasurer; the sixth member is the President of the Los Angeles Board of Education or his/her designee; and the seventh member, called the public member, is appointed by the Board of Education. The Annuity Reserve Fund Board administers as trustees a portfolio of approximately $30 million for the benefit of defined certificated former and present employees of this School District.

It seems to me that we are, indeed, people who manage public investments. However, we are not designated in our agency's conflict of interest code and are uncertain about our status as public investment managers. Accordingly, we are deferring filing statements of economic interest pending your advice.

I look forward to hearing from you at your earliest opportunity.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
Robert Booker

RB:vj1
Enclosure

c: Members, Annuity Reserve Fund Board
    Mr. Ron Apperson
